name: Jira
source: Direct access — 141 projects, 313 boards
notes: Universal work system. Criticality: Red/High per Cake Engineering Tools page. Managed by Cloud services team.
pricing_notes: Cloud Premium ~$14–17/user/mo for ~370 users. Bundled Atlassian suite.
teams_using: All engineering; CS Implementations; Operations; PMO
interview_question: 141 projects and 18,583 open issues — is anyone tracking the grooming cadence across all of these?

name: Confluence
source: Direct access — 165 spaces, 14,111+ pages indexed
notes: Active documentation culture. Criticality: Red/High. Managed by Cloud services team.
pricing_notes: Bundled with Jira licensing.
interview_question: Confluence has 14,000+ pages. Is Guru also actively used, or has it been superseded?

name: Bitbucket Cloud
source: Direct access — 4 workspaces, 3,191 repos
notes: Four workspaces (madmobile, syscolabs, madpayments, syscolabsconf). Criticality: Red/High. 75% of repos stale (no commits in 90d).
pricing_notes: Bundled with Atlassian. Build minutes may be separate cost.
interview_question: GitHub is also listed in the engineering tools page with a note 'Can we get rid of this?' — what's the status?

name: GitHub
source: Cake Engineering Tools Confluence page — listed as Red/High criticality, managed by Leads. Note: 'Can we get rid of this?'
notes: OVERLAP: Running alongside Bitbucket. Internal page already questions whether it should be decommissioned.
pricing_notes: May be legacy. If still paying, potential immediate savings.
interview_question: Is the GitHub org still active, or has everything moved to Bitbucket? Are you still paying for it?

name: AWS
source: SSO access — 18 accounts
notes: Multi-account org with ControlTower. Top accounts: Mgmt $250K, Monvia $23K, CAKE Dev $15K, Retail Prod US $12K.
pricing_notes: Consolidated billing through mm-madmobile-mgmt. Includes $58K/mo Savings Plans, $17K/mo Enterprise Support.
interview_question: The AWS Cost Savings 2025 doc identifies $7K/mo in EBS snapshot savings alone. How much of that has been captured?

name: Grafana (self-hosted)
source: AWS inventory — Shared Services EKS cluster. $1,813/mo in mm-shared-services.
notes: OVERLAP: 6 observability tools identified. Self-hosted Mimir (metrics), Loki (logs), Tempo (traces). Payments has its own instance.
pricing_notes: OSS, no license fee. Cost is EKS + S3 storage for Mimir/Loki/Tempo data.
overlap_with: Datadog; Nagios; Munin; Graylog; DB Cacti
interview_question: I count 6 monitoring/observability tools in the stack. What's the consolidation plan?

name: Datadog
source: Confluence — 'Datadog Reference' (MenuPad), 'Concierge Core -- Datadog Integration Effort'. URL: https://app.datadoghq.com
notes: OVERLAP: Running alongside self-hosted Grafana stack. Used for Real User Monitoring and Log Explorer per Confluence. Scope and contract unknown — key ask for Ana.
pricing_notes: APM + RUM + Log Management. Highly variable based on hosts, custom metrics, log volume.
interview_question: Datadog is running for Concierge/MenuPad alongside the Grafana stack for everything else. Is there a plan to consolidate, or do they serve different purposes?

name: Nagios
source: Cake Engineering Tools page — 'Nagios - Monitor and alert based on threshold values'. Managed by Cloud services team.
notes: OVERLAP: Legacy monitoring alongside Grafana and Datadog.
pricing_notes: OSS or commercial. EC2 hosting cost included in AWS.
interview_question: Is Nagios still actively used, or has Grafana replaced it?

name: Munin
source: Cake Engineering Tools page — 'Munin - To monitor servers'. Low criticality. Managed by Cloud services team.
notes: OVERLAP: Server monitoring that may be superseded by Grafana Mimir.
pricing_notes: Open source server monitoring.

name: Graylog
source: Cake Engineering Tools page — 'Graylog servers'. Low criticality. Managed by Leads/Cloud services.
notes: OVERLAP: Log management alongside Loki and Datadog Log Explorer.
pricing_notes: OSS or commercial log management.

name: DB Cacti
source: Cake Engineering Tools page — 'DB Cacti - To monitor servers'. Low criticality. Managed by DB Leads.
notes: OVERLAP: Database-focused monitoring.
pricing_notes: Open source network/server monitoring.

name: Jenkins
source: AWS: 9 Jenkins instances in mm-cake-development (dev-qa-payment-jenkins, pos-db-jenkins, qa-jenkins-master, etc.)
notes: OVERLAP: Dual CI/CD. Criticality: Red/High per Cake Eng Tools. Managed by Leads/Cloud services. Tooling Evaluation doc explicitly compared Jenkins vs Pipelines vs GitLab — Jenkins scored lower due to 'hassle of maintaining cluster' and 'runtime conflicts'.
cost_detail: ~$1,500–2,500/month estimated EC2 cost (9 instances in CAKE Dev)
pricing_notes: OSS, no license. Cost is EC2 compute for 9 instances (master + slaves + ZAP).
interview_question: The tooling evaluation doc scored Jenkins lower than Bitbucket Pipelines due to cluster maintenance overhead. Is there a timeline to consolidate?

name: Bitbucket Pipelines
source: Bitbucket inventory — pipeline configs in repos, 87% coverage in madpayments
notes: OVERLAP: Dual CI/CD with Jenkins. Preferred tool per Tooling Evaluation (scored 3-4 on criteria vs Jenkins 3).
estimated_annual_cost: Included in Atlassian + build minute overages
pricing_notes: Included in Bitbucket licensing. Build minutes may incur overages.

name: Terraform
source: AWS inventory — state buckets across multiple accounts
notes: IaC standard. Payments uses extensively (TF state in mm-payments-prod-us).
pricing_notes: If using OSS CLI only: free. If Terraform Cloud/Enterprise: per-user pricing.
interview_question: Are you using Terraform OSS or Terraform Cloud/Enterprise? That's a meaningful cost difference.

name: Cursor
source: Bitbucket — .cursor/rules in 2 repos (seao, restaurant-admin-v2). Confluence — 'Design Integration with Cursor', 'API Automation using Cursor', 'Agentic Lovable Dev Flow', 'Test Automation with Cursor Tool'.
notes: AI space Tools page shows <5 adoption. Multiple Confluence pages show active experimentation (Figma MCP, API automation, agentic dev flow).
pricing_notes: $20/mo Pro, $40/mo Business per seat.

name: Claude / Anthropic

notes: 8 of 30 scanned repos have AI tooling config (26.7% adoption rate).
pricing_notes: API usage-based or included in Cursor subscription.
